source.page_label: Hunting Seasons | Missouri Department of Conservation
source.update_note: 2026-27 upland game bird data from MDC seasons page. Missouri has quail, pheasant, and turkey.

species.name: Wild Turkey (Fall)
species.season_raw: Fall Firearms: Oct 1-31; Fall Archery: Sep 15-Nov 13 & Nov 25-Jan 15

licenses.name: Small Game Hunting Permit
licenses.covers: Quail, pheasant, rabbit, squirrel, and other small game

licenses.name: Spring Turkey Hunting Permit
licenses.covers: Spring turkey season (1 or 2 bearded turkeys)

licenses.name: Archer's Hunting Permit
licenses.covers: Deer archery + small game + furbearers; includes fall turkey archery

key_notes.title: Missouri residents and resident landowners may harvest 2 male/bearded turkeys in spring. Nonresidents may harvest 1. Only 1 in the first week.

sources.label: MDC — Spring Turkey Permits
sources.evidence: Missouri residents and nonresident landowners may harvest two male turkeys or turkeys with visible beard. Nonresident hunters may harvest one bearded turkey. Only one turkey may be taken during the first week.

key_notes.title: Fall turkey firearms season is not allowed in Dunklin, McDonald, Mississippi, New Madrid, Newton, Pemiscot, and Scott counties. Archery is statewide.

sources.label: MDC — Fall Turkey Permits
sources.evidence: During the firearms portion of fall turkey season, you may hunt turkeys with firearms except in Dunklin, McDonald, Mississippi, New Madrid, Newton, Pemiscot, and Scott counties.

key_notes.title: Pheasant hunting is limited primarily to northern Missouri. Only cock (male) pheasants may be taken.

sources.label: MDC — Pheasant Season
sources.evidence: Pheasant: Nov 1, 2026 - Jan 15, 2027. Daily limit: 2 cocks.
